items.title: Chopper pilot and police officer honoured for heroic Katherine flood rescue

children.content: A helicopter pilot and a police officer who performed a daring flood rescue in Katherine earlier this year have been awarded for their heroics as part of the Northern Territory's Territory Day awards.

image.altText: A male police officer, with a lot of medals pinned to his uniform, standing outside and smiling.

items.title: NT power, water and sewerage prices increase by 5.3 per cent from today

children.content: The Northern Territory treasurer has defended an increase in power, water and sewerage prices, which are set to rise by 5.3 per cent from today.

image.altText: Power lines on a leafy green street.

items.title: Today in history: NT town of Katherine gazetted 100 years ago

children.content: Today marks 100 years of the NT town of Katherine, which has a rich history as a meeting place and crossroads to the north and as home to some of Australia's most stunning natural sites.

A bronze statue of a man on a horse on a red concrete base
A bridge in the process of being built in 1924

items.title: 'Recurring issues' uncovered following surveillance of chopper sector

children.content: Australia's aviation watchdog has warned that cutting corners on maintenance and certification requirements could create a "real safety risk".

image.altText: A chopper shot from below, it is parked on green grass and a blue sky can be seen in the background.

items.title: 'Hyperscale' data centre planned for remote NT

children.content: The proposed 19,150-hectare 'Project Ares' would be built by Energy North on Murranji Station in the Barkly Region, as up to 12 proponents eye the NT for data centres.

image.altText: a gate with a cut-out of a man on a horse silhouetted against a sunset.

items.title: Teenagers say vapes are still commonplace and easily accessible

children.content: The ABC has spoken to a number of teenagers who say despite a crack down on vapes, they remain accessible and commonplace.

image.altText: Disposable vapes sitting on a countertop.

items.title: Study finds NT residents are the nation's happiest

children.content: Northern Territory residents are the happiest in the nation, a new university study has found, with access to nature, warm weather and cultural diversity among the theories for Territorians' sunny attitudes.

image.altText: A woman, smiling, standing in a CBD mall.

items.title: Jillaroos, jackaroos and an iconic event: The Brunette Races in pictures

children.content: More than 800 people from across northern Australia hit a remote outback cattle station over the weekend for the iconic annual Brunette Races.

items.title: Court finds staffer's relationship with 20yo parolee 'grossly inappropriate'

children.content: An Alice Springs parole worker found in a hotel room with the 20-year-old she was responsible for supporting has avoided prison for attempting to pervert the course of justice.

image.altText: A woman outside the court hides her face behind a cardigan, supporters around her attempt to block the view with umbrellas.

items.title: 'Kick in the guts': NT veterans join calls for health funding cap removal

children.content: The federal government wants to cap access to physical and mental health supports to $5,000 a year for injured war veterans, but there are growing concerns the move will push some into "a worse-off situation".

image.altText: A group of people sitting around a table in a cafe, an older man is pointing his finger as he speaks to the group.

children.content: The Socceroos' World Cup adventure will either end or become historic when they face Egypt on Friday.

articles.title: French unity on display as their ruthless run through World Cup continues

children.content: In a French team littered with superstars it is something intangible that could stop them taking another World Cup title, following another clinical display against Sweden in the round of 32.

items.title: PM sidesteps question on whether house prices will continue falling

children.content: Anthony Albanese has sidestepped questions on whether house prices will continue falling, following the release of data today showing a 0.4 per cent drop in home values. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese raises his hand while speaking in parliament.

items.title: Trillionaire Elon Musk called out at royal commission for antisemitism on X

children.content: An online hate prevention charity which monitors and reports antisemitic content says trillionaire Elon Musk is partly to blame for anti-Jewish hatred on social media platform X.

image.altText: Elon Musk attends the US-Saudi Investment Forum in Washington, DC

items.title: Vanuatu deal is significant but a harbinger of what's to come in the Pacific

children.content: Although a big moment in diplomacy, the Vanuatu agreement has come at a clear reputational cost to Australia.

image.altText: Jotham Napat and Anthony Albanese hold up signed documents.

items.title: ACCC blocks Coles supermarket to protect regional city's independents

children.content: In an unprecedented move, Australia's consumer watchdog has exercised new powers to block the opening of a Coles supermarket, on the grounds it could knock out smaller competitors in the surrounding region.

image.altText: The owner of a supermarket standing in fruit and vegetable isle with arms crossed.

items.title: Why Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams before their match

children.content: Australian Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams in the days leading up to their Wimbledon opener just to remove some of the mythos around the 23-time grand slam champion.

articles.title: Who is Maya Joint, Australian conqueror of Serena at Wimbledon?

children.content: Maya Joint was born in April 2006 when Serena Williams had already won seven Grand Slam singles titles. So who is the 20-year-old who just defeated a tennis legend in her return to the All England Club?

image.altText: A red-headed female tennis player in white pokes tongue out on Wimbledon centre court

articles.title: Australian rising star takes down Serena Williams in Wimbledon return

children.content: Australian Maya Joint holds her nerve to beat tennis legend Serena Williams in the 23-time grand slam champion's first professional tennis match in four years.

image.altText: Maya Joint shakes Serena Williams's hand over the net at Wimbledon.

items.title: The condition that causes people to get lost in their own home

children.content: Some estimates suggest up to one in 30 people may be affected by a condition that has been described as a lifelong inability to orient oneself — even in extremely familiar surroundings.

items.title: Canberra man pleads guilty to performing Nazi salutes in public

children.content: A Canberra man has pleaded guilty after performing Nazi salutes at two suburban shopping centres and placing white supremacist stickers over Indigenous and multicultural signs at the Australian National University.

image.altText: A young man leaves court.

items.title: Childcare provider stripped of funding labels decision 'devastating'

children.content: A Victorian childcare provider stripped of its federal government funding over unresolved safety breaches has vowed to challenge the decision.

items.title: Treatment helping alleged sexual abuse victims also denies them justice

items.title: Alcoa to buy South32's WA bauxite mine in $8b deal

children.content: American mining giant Alcoa is set to buy $8 billion worth of assets from Australia's South32. The local shire and workers' union say they were blindsided by the deal.

items.title: Country-of-origin labelling laws set to rewrite seafood menus

image.altText: image of imported and australian fish on display in shop

children.content: New labelling laws for hospitality venues serving seafood should help diners know exactly what they are eating.

items.title: Coalition avoids direct One Nation messaging in daily talking points

children.content: Coalition talking points, obtained by the ABC, make almost no direct mention of One Nation despite the minor party surging in the polls.

items.title: Certain sports have a high risk of brain injury. What can you do as a parent?

children.content: When your child decides they are keen on playing sport, there are lots of factors to consider as a parent, and after recent news stories about chronic traumatic encephalopathy, keeping them safe from head injuries may be front of mind.

children.content: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating two earthquakes has risen to more than 1,900 as tens of thousands of people still remain unaccounted for.

items.title: Woman raises awareness of farmers' mental health after husband's death

items.title: 'Not fair and honest': Accounting firms face split after KPMG scandal

children.content: Accounting firms could be asked to split their lucrative consulting services from their audit functions and individual firm partners could face far greater scrutiny and penalties for legal breaches, under a shake up of the industry being consulted on by the federal government.
